text.title: Support Tool Pm Triage Spec Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Support Tool Pm Triage Spec. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Tag taxonomy has EXACTLY 5 categories: bug / feature-request / billing / docs / praise.
rule.rationale: Tag taxonomy has EXACTLY 5 categories: bug / feature-request / billing / docs / praise.

rule.statement: Escalation: bug+p0 → drop-everything; feature ×3 same week → triage; billing → immediate.
rule.rationale: Escalation: bug+p0 → drop-everything; feature ×3 same week → triage; billing → immediate.

rule.statement: Weekly digest: top 5 quotes per category + counts.
rule.rationale: Weekly digest: top 5 quotes per category + counts.

rule.statement: Each ticket has a named human owner; bots are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Each ticket has a named human owner; bots are forbidden.

rule.statement: Tickets older than 90 days archived; tag stays for trend analysis.
rule.rationale: Tickets older than 90 days archived; tag stays for trend analysis.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-support-tool-pm-triage-spec.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
